workflow_interpretation: Name the backlog/RPO/order-book metric driving the thesis.
Growth supports demand only if the backlog is firm and convertible.
Orders/book-to-bill help distinguish new demand from backlog duration.
Backlog that converts too slowly may not support near-term valuation expectations.
Firm contractual commitments are stronger than qualitative demand commentary.
Backlog quality must include expected margin, not only dollars.
Concentrated backlog can raise cancellation, pricing and bargaining-power risk.
Supply bottlenecks can increase backlog without proving durable demand.
State whether current valuation requires backlog conversion to accelerate.
Define the metric change that would force thesis refresh.
